messages.content: You are an AI Assistant for an automotive engineering company. You assist employees, business partners, vendors, customers, and internal engineering teams with accurate, professional, and helpful responses. You have knowledge of HR policies, procurement processes, product and warranty information, and engineering documentation including Technical Service Bulletins, specifications, and compliance standards. Always be concise, factual, and guide users to the right next step where applicable.
I have a question about my vehicle — is there a courtesy car available while my vehicle is being serviced? Any guidance would be helpful.

Of course. Many of our authorised dealers offer courtesy vehicles subject to availability. Please request a courtesy car when booking your service appointment. A valid driving licence and insurance are required. Some dealers offer a collection and delivery service as an alternative — ask when booking.

Can I visit your facilities as part of a business partnership? Could you clarify this for me?

Thank you for your query. Business partner site visits are arranged through your designated Procurement or Partnership Manager. All visitors must complete our visitor induction, sign an NDA if applicable, and comply with site health and safety rules. Please provide at least 5 business days' notice for visits.

How are changes to an existing contract managed? Any guidance would be helpful.

Happy to help. Contract changes must be agreed in writing via a formal Contract Amendment signed by authorised representatives from both parties. Verbal agreements or email-only changes are not binding. Submit proposed amendments to your Procurement Manager for review and legal approval before implementation.

I need to know: what training is available for new employees? Please let me know how to proceed.

New employees complete a structured onboarding programme in their first 30 days, including mandatory compliance training, a company overview, role-specific technical training, and an introduction to key systems. A dedicated buddy is assigned for the first 90 days. Training records are maintained in the HRIS portal.

I have a question about my vehicle — what roadside assistance is available? Thanks in advance.

All new vehicle purchases include 3 years of 24/7 roadside assistance. The service covers breakdown recovery, flat tyre assistance, fuel delivery, battery jump-start, and emergency accommodation if you cannot reach your destination. Call 0800-XXX-XXXX or use the owner app to request assistance.

Can I carry over unused annual leave to next year?

Of course. Up to 5 days of unused annual leave may be carried over to the following calendar year, subject to line manager and HR approval. Carried-over leave must be used by the end of Q1 of the following year. Leave beyond the 5-day carry-over limit will be forfeited unless exceptional circumstances are approved by HR.

My vehicle software needs an update. How is this done? Any guidance would be helpful.

Great question. Software updates are delivered over-the-air (OTA) via your vehicle's connected services when connected to Wi-Fi. You will receive a notification on the infotainment screen. Schedule the update for a time when the vehicle is parked and connected. Alternatively, updates can be applied by your authorised dealer during a scheduled service visit.

Hi, I'm an employee and what is the policy on secondary employment? Please let me know how to proceed.

Of course. Secondary employment is permitted only with prior written approval from HR and your line manager. It must not conflict with your primary role, create a conflict of interest, or affect your performance. Approval can be withdrawn if any of these conditions are breached.

Our company needs to know: how are supplier performance reviews conducted? Thanks in advance.

Great question. Supplier performance is reviewed quarterly across four metrics: on-time delivery, quality (PPM), responsiveness, and commercial compliance. Results are shared via the Supplier Portal. Consistently underperforming suppliers enter a Supplier Improvement Programme, and persistent issues may result in de-listing.

I just bought your car and how do I book a service appointment? Thanks in advance.

Of course. You can book a service appointment through our website at service.company.com, via the owner mobile app, or by calling your nearest authorised dealer directly. Provide your vehicle registration number and a description of any issues. We recommend booking at least 1 week in advance for routine services.

I need to know: is there a referral bonus for recommending new hires? Thanks in advance.

Certainly. Yes. The employee referral programme pays a bonus for successful hires referred by current employees. The standard bonus is paid in two tranches: 50% on the new hire's start date and 50% after the new hire completes 6 months. Amounts vary by role level and are published on the HR intranet.

Technical question: what is the calibration schedule for torque tools on the assembly line? Please let me know how to proceed.

Of course. Torque tools used on safety-critical fasteners must be calibrated every 6 months or after any tool drop or suspected damage, whichever is sooner. Calibration must be performed by an approved metrology lab and records kept in the Calibration Management System. Tools out of calibration are tagged and removed from service immediately. See Quality Procedure QP-CAL-003.

Our company needs to know: how do I register as a new vendor? Thanks in advance.

Of course. To register as a new vendor, complete the Vendor Registration Form on our Supplier Portal at suppliers.company.com. You will need to provide your company registration documents, VAT number, bank details, and contact information. Once submitted, our Procurement team will verify your details and respond within 5 business days.

How do I activate connected services on my vehicle? Please let me know how to proceed.

Connected services are activated via the owner app or at owners.company.com. Create an account, enter your VIN, and follow the setup wizard. Connected services include remote locking, live traffic updates, vehicle health reports, and over-the-air software updates. A subscription may be required after the initial complimentary period.

Of course. The company follows a smart-casual dress code for office-based work. Engineering and workshop areas require PPE as specified by the Health & Safety policy. Client-facing roles may require business formal attire on designated days. Your line manager will advise on specific requirements for your team.

As a customer, how often should I service my vehicle? Please let me know how to proceed.

Of course. We recommend servicing your vehicle every 12 months or 10,000 miles, whichever comes first. Your vehicle's service indicator will alert you when a service is due. Adhering to the service schedule is important for maintaining your warranty and vehicle performance.

As a vendor, what sustainability requirements do you have for suppliers? Could you clarify this for me?

Of course. We require all Tier 1 suppliers to complete our annual Sustainability Self-Assessment covering carbon emissions, waste management, and ethical labour practices. Suppliers are expected to align with the UN Global Compact principles. A Supplier Code of Conduct is available on the Supplier Portal and must be acknowledged annually.

Quick question — what is the home office allowance for remote workers? Any guidance would be helpful.

Happy to help. Remote workers are entitled to a one-time home office setup allowance of $750, plus a monthly contribution toward home internet and utilities. Claims must be submitted via the expense system with receipts. Full details are in HR-POL-003 Section 6 and HR-POL-004 Section 5.6.

Great question. You can reach our customer support team by phone at 0800-XXX-XXXX (Mon–Fri 8am–6pm), by email at support@company.com, or via live chat on our website. For urgent vehicle safety issues, our 24/7 line is available. We aim to respond to all queries within 1 business day.

I have a question about my vehicle — can I take my car to an independent garage for servicing? Could you clarify this for me?

Of course. Your vehicle can be serviced at an independent garage without invalidating the warranty, provided they use parts that meet our specifications and keep full service records. However, for warranty claims, we recommend using an authorised dealer to avoid any disputes over parts or labour standards.

As a customer, what fuel type does my vehicle require? Any guidance would be helpful.

The recommended fuel type for your vehicle is printed on the inside of the fuel cap and in the owner's manual. Most of our petrol models require 95 RON unleaded or higher. Using incorrect fuel can cause damage not covered under warranty. If you have accidentally misfuelled, do not start the engine — call roadside assistance immediately.

Great question. Invoices must be submitted electronically to invoices@company.com or via the Supplier Portal. Each invoice must reference a valid Purchase Order number. Invoices without a PO reference will be returned unpaid. Ensure your VAT registration number, company address, and banking details are correct on the invoice.

What accessories are available for my vehicle? Any guidance would be helpful.

Thank you for your query. A full range of genuine accessories is available through your authorised dealer or our online accessories store at accessories.company.com. Genuine accessories are designed specifically for your vehicle and do not affect your warranty. Popular items include roof racks, tow bars, all-weather mats, and dash cameras. Enter your VIN to see compatible options.

Need engineering guidance — what NVH targets apply to the new platform?

Thank you for your query. NVH targets for the new platform are defined in the Vehicle Attribute Specification (VAS) document for your platform. Key interior noise targets are: motorway cruise (120 km/h) ≤67 dB(A), idle ≤42 dB(A). Full target cascade is in VAS Section 8. Contact the NVH CAE team for simulation models and the Chassis Dynamics team for physical test correlation requirements.

I want to request a mental health day. Is that covered? Any guidance would be helpful.

Certainly. Yes. The company provides 2 paid Mental Health Days per calendar year, separate from your sick leave entitlement. These are recorded in the HRIS portal under 'Mental Health Leave' and do not require a medical certificate. They are available to all full-time and part-time employees. See HR-POL-001 Section 4.2 and HR-POL-004 Section 5.4 for full details.

Details are available on the HR intranet or by contacting HR directly.

Great question. Suppliers must carry minimum public liability insurance of £5 million and product liability insurance of £10 million. Suppliers working on-site must additionally carry employer's liability insurance of £10 million. Certificates of insurance must be provided annually and upon request.

Of course. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Great question. OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

What is the fuel system leak test specification? Any guidance would be helpful.

Great question. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification?

Of course. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Thank you for your query. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Technical question: what is the fuel system leak test specification? Thanks in advance.

Thank you for your query. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Certainly. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Happy to help. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Of course. EV battery packs must be validated to ECE R100 (electric power train safety) and UN 38.3 (transport of lithium batteries). All test reports must be filed in the Compliance Vault before vehicle type approval.

Need engineering guidance — what is the specification for brake fluid used in our vehicles? Could you clarify this for me?

Great question. Our vehicles require DOT 4 brake fluid meeting SAE J1703 and FMVSS 116 standards. The specified fluid is detailed in the Workshop Manual under Brake Systems > Fluid Specifications. Never mix DOT grades. Brake fluid should be replaced every 2 years or 30,000 miles. Only use fluid from our Approved Parts List to ensure warranty and compliance.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification? Could you clarify this for me?

Certainly. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Technical question: what is the calibration schedule for torque tools on the assembly line? Could you clarify this for me?

Torque tools used on safety-critical fasteners must be calibrated every 6 months or after any tool drop or suspected damage, whichever is sooner. Calibration must be performed by an approved metrology lab and records kept in the Calibration Management System. Tools out of calibration are tagged and removed from service immediately. See Quality Procedure QP-CAL-003.

I'm an engineer and what cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow? Any guidance would be helpful.

Thank you for your query. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

How is over-the-air software update validation conducted? Any guidance would be helpful.

Of course. OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

Of course. Homologation for a new market variant follows a four-stage process: (1) Regulatory gap analysis against target market standards, (2) Engineering adaptation and testing to meet local requirements, (3) Type Approval submission to the relevant authority, and (4) Certificate of Conformity issuance. Lead time is typically 12–18 months. Contact the Homologation team at homologation@company.com to initiate.

Can I modify my vehicle without voiding the warranty?

Certainly. Modifications that do not affect vehicle systems or safety (such as cosmetic changes) generally do not void the warranty. However, modifications to the engine, suspension, brakes, software, or emissions systems may void the relevant warranty components. Contact your dealer before making any modifications to understand the potential warranty implications.

What is the approved weld process for chassis structural repairs?

Chassis structural welds must be performed to our Weld Specification WS-CHASSIS-002, which specifies MIG welding with ER70S-6 wire, pre-heat requirements, and post-weld inspection criteria. Only certified welders with current BS EN ISO 9606-1 qualification may perform structural repairs. All repairs must be inspected by a Level 2 NDT technician before sign-off.

I'm an engineer and what cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow?

Great question. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

See Quality Procedure QP-CAL-003.

What cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow? Any guidance would be helpful.

Certainly. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Technical question: what is the fuel system leak test specification? Thanks in advance.

Of course. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

I'm an engineer and what is the specification for brake fluid used in our vehicles? Any guidance would be helpful.

Our vehicles require DOT 4 brake fluid meeting SAE J1703 and FMVSS 116 standards. The specified fluid is detailed in the Workshop Manual under Brake Systems > Fluid Specifications. Never mix DOT grades. Brake fluid should be replaced every 2 years or 30,000 miles. Only use fluid from our Approved Parts List to ensure warranty and compliance.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification? Any guidance would be helpful.

Happy to help. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification? Could you clarify this for me?

Happy to help. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

Technical question: what is the fuel system leak test specification? Please let me know how to proceed.

Of course. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

I'm an engineer and how is over-the-air software update validation conducted? Could you clarify this for me?

OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

Technical question: what cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow? Thanks in advance.

Thank you for your query. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification?

Happy to help. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

Great question. Cylinder head bolt torque specifications for the 2.0T engine are: Stage 1 — 20 Nm, Stage 2 — 60 Nm, Stage 3 — 90° angle tightening. I'm an engineer and what cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow? Any guidance would be helpful.

Certainly. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Need engineering guidance — what is the approved weld process for chassis structural repairs? Could you clarify this for me?

Chassis structural welds must be performed to our Weld Specification WS-CHASSIS-002, which specifies MIG welding with ER70S-6 wire, pre-heat requirements, and post-weld inspection criteria. Only certified welders with current BS EN ISO 9606-1 qualification may perform structural repairs. All repairs must be inspected by a Level 2 NDT technician before sign-off.

What is the fuel system leak test specification? Thanks in advance.

Happy to help. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Technical question: what cooling system pressure test procedure should I follow? Could you clarify this for me?

Of course. The cooling system pressure test procedure is detailed in Workshop Manual Chapter 7, Section 7.4. Test at 1.4 bar (20 psi) using a calibrated pressure tester. Hold for 10 minutes and check for pressure drop. Any drop exceeding 0.1 bar indicates a leak. Check hoses, clamps, radiator, heater matrix, and head gasket. Document test results on the Vehicle Test Record form.

Need engineering guidance — what is the fuel system leak test specification? Could you clarify this for me?

Thank you for your query. Fuel system leak testing is specified in Test Standard TS-FUEL-004. Static leak test: pressurise to 0.35 bar and hold for 5 minutes with zero permissible pressure drop. Dynamic test: run engine at idle for 10 minutes and inspect all connections with leak detection fluid. Any leak is a fail. Document all results on the Fuel System Test Record and escalate failures to the Fuel Systems Lead.

Need engineering guidance — how is over-the-air software update validation conducted? Any guidance would be helpful.

Of course. OTA software updates go through a five-stage validation process: (1) Unit testing on hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench, (2) Integration testing on a full vehicle rig, (3) Regression testing against all affected ECUs, (4) Cybersecurity assessment to ISO/SAE 21434, and (5) Fleet pilot with 100 vehicles for 4 weeks. Full release follows successful pilot with no P0/P1 defects outstanding.

What is considered sexual harassment?

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.

- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.

- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

What protection is given to employees who report harassment?

The policy establishes the following:

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.

- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.
- Reports can be made to: direct manager, HR Business Partner, anonymous Ethics Hotline (ethics-hotline@company.com), or Legal/Compliance.
- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

My team member wants to take annual leave next week — what is the minimum notice they should have given?

For your reference as a manager,

- Full-time employees are entitled to a minimum of 20 working days of paid annual leave per calendar year.
- Annual leave accrues at 1.67 days per month.
- Leave must be requested through the HRIS portal at least 10 working days in advance for absences of 3 or more days.
- Shorter absences require reasonable advance notice where practicable.
- Where local statutory provisions exceed these minimums, local law prevails.

What are the social media guidelines in Section 8 of the Code of Conduct?

The policy establishes the following:

- Employees must exercise judgment when making public statements on personal social media that could be associated with the company.
- Do not disclose confidential or proprietary information.
- Do not make statements that could be construed as the company's official position unless authorized.
- Do not make disparaging statements about colleagues, clients, or competitors.
- Personal opinions must be clearly identified as your own, not the company's.

Source document: HR-POL-002, Section 8.

What is the difference between harassment and misconduct under our policies?

As set out in our HR documentation,

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.
- Examples include offensive jokes, slurs, unwanted physical contact, threats, and intimidation.
- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.
- Reports can be made to: direct manager, HR Business Partner, anonymous Ethics Hotline (ethics-hotline@company.com), or Legal/Compliance.

For your records, the full policy is in HR-POL-002, Section 5.

- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

At what point do I need to issue a formal verbal warning for absences?

For your reference as a manager,

- The disciplinary framework applies to unscheduled absences (leave occurrences).
- 3rd occurrence: Informal Discussion.
- 5th occurrence: Formal Verbal Warning, documented in personnel file.
- 7th occurrence: Written Warning.
- 9th or subsequent occurrence: Final Written Warning or Dismissal.
- This framework covers attendance matters only; conduct matters are governed by HR-POL-002.

You can find the full details in HR-POL-001, Section 6.

Is it possible to report harassment anonymously?

What is the difference between harassment and misconduct under our policies?

Company policy provides the following guidance:

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.
- Examples include offensive jokes, slurs, unwanted physical contact, threats, and intimidation.
- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.
- Reports can be made to: direct manager, HR Business Partner, anonymous Ethics Hotline (ethics-hotline@company.com), or Legal/Compliance.
- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.

- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.
- Reports can be made to: direct manager, HR Business Partner, anonymous Ethics Hotline (ethics-hotline@company.com), or Legal/Compliance.
- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

What protection is given to employees who report harassment?

Company policy (referenced below) provides:

- Harassment is any unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment.
- Examples include offensive jokes, slurs, unwanted physical contact, threats, and intimidation.
- Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and conduct of a sexual nature affecting employment conditions.
- Reports can be made to: direct manager, HR Business Partner, anonymous Ethics Hotline (ethics-hotline@company.com), or Legal/Compliance.
- Retaliation against reporters is itself a violation of policy.

Source document: HR-POL-002, Section 5.

For further information, consult HR-POL-004, Section 5.5.

At what point should we request medical documentation for sick leave?

The policy establishes the following:

- Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per calendar year.
- Sick leave does not accrue and does not carry over year to year.
- Medical documentation may be required for absences exceeding three consecutive days.
- Additionally, employees receive 2 paid Mental Health Days per year, separate from sick leave (see HR-POL-004, Section 5.4).

Policy reference: HR-POL-001, Section 4.2.

Walk me through the full attendance disciplinary framework.

Per HR-POL-001, Section 6:

- The disciplinary framework applies to unscheduled absences (leave occurrences).
- 3rd occurrence: Informal Discussion.
- 5th occurrence: Formal Verbal Warning, documented in personnel file.
- 7th occurrence: Written Warning.
- 9th or subsequent occurrence: Final Written Warning or Dismissal.
- This framework covers attendance matters only; conduct matters are governed by HR-POL-002.

Policy reference: HR-POL-001, Section 6.

Note: This framework covers attendance matters only. Conduct matters are subject to the disciplinary framework in HR-POL-002.

How is my salary determined?

Based on company policy,

- All roles are classified within a defined job grade with a salary band (minimum, midpoint, maximum).
- The company targets the 50th to 75th percentile of the relevant market.
- Salary bands are reviewed periodically to maintain market alignment.

See HR-POL-004, Section 3.1 for the complete policy text.

Are mental health days the same as sick days?

- Employees receive up to 2 paid Mental Health Days per year.
- Mental health days are separate from the 10-day sick leave entitlement.

Does parental leave apply to surrogacy?

Under company policy,

- Primary caregivers receive up to 16 weeks of paid leave following birth, adoption, or surrogacy.
- Secondary caregivers receive up to 4 weeks of paid leave.
- Employees must provide at least 30 days advance notice where reasonably practicable.
- Parental leave rights under FMLA and equivalent local legislation run concurrently.

For further information, consult HR-POL-001, Section 4.3 and HR-POL-004, Section 5.6.
